text: Direct electrochemical propylene epoxidation using water-derived intermediates offers a sustainable and safer alternative to conventional industrial processes that rely on hazardous chlorine or peroxide reagents. In this study, we report an oxidized palladium-platinum alloy catalyst (PdPtOₓ/C) capable of achieving a Faradaic efficiency of 66 ± 5% for propylene oxide production at 50 mA cm⁻² under ambient conditions. By embedding platinum into the palladium oxide lattice, we successfully stabilized oxidized platinum species, significantly enhancing the catalyst’s overall performance and durability. Kinetic analyses indicate that epoxidation on the PdPtOₓ/C surface proceeds via an electrophilic attack by metal-bound peroxo intermediates. These findings establish a robust strategy for the selective electrochemical transfer of oxygen atoms directly from water, providing a versatile platform for diverse oxygenation reactions without the need for chemical mediators.
